notes: Grounding is live web search against real professors; no fixture KB. Assertion kinds: file = a written knowledge-base file; chat = the assistant's reply; schema = output passes shared/schemas/validate_kb.py.

evals.name: strong-fit-rl-robotics
evals.prompt: I'm finishing a master's in computer science and my research is on reinforcement learning for robotics — mostly sim-to-real transfer, and I have one workshop paper on domain randomization. I'm applying for PhDs starting in 2027. Can you do a deep dive on Professor Sergey Levine at UC Berkeley and tell me whether he'd be a good PhD fit for me? If he's a fit, I'd want to know what to mention if I email him.
evals.expected_output: A structured professor profile in knowledge-base/professors/ with recent publications (with takeaways), agenda/open problems, funding/lab signals, a justified numeric fit score, and 2-4 concrete outreach hooks grounded in his real recent work. Strong, well-evidenced fit with real citations.

assertions.text: Writes knowledge-base/professors/<slug>.md with all schema front-matter fields filled (blank not absent)
Output file passes shared/schemas/validate_kb.py with no errors
fit_score is high (>=70) and justified by named overlaps, not asserted
Has ## Recent publications, ## Fit with my profile, ## Outreach hooks, ## Sources sections
enrichment field records provenance (openalex/arxiv/websearch/none)
Chat reply gives the fit verdict, the strongest hook, and any gap
No fabricated papers; flags any unavailable enrichment honestly

evals.name: field-agnostic-biochem
evals.prompt: Look into Professor Jennifer Doudna's lab at UC Berkeley for a PhD. My background is biochemistry — I did two years of undergrad research on lipid nanoparticle delivery of CRISPR components and I'm interested in gene-editing therapeutics. Is this a good lab for me and what would I want to highlight in outreach?
evals.expected_output: A structured profile for a life-sciences PI demonstrating the skill is field-agnostic (bio-appropriate sources, not just arXiv). Recent work with takeaways, agenda, fit vs. the applicant's CRISPR-delivery background, concrete hooks, real publications.

assertions.text: Writes a schema-conformant professor file (passes validate_kb.py)
Grounds in life-sciences sources (PubMed/Europe PMC/OpenAlex), not just arXiv
Fit assessment names the CRISPR-delivery overlap specifically
Outreach hooks reference real recent Doudna-lab work
Chat reply summarizes fit and the strongest hook

evals.name: weak-fit-honesty
evals.prompt: I want to do a PhD in natural language processing, specifically multilingual large language models and low-resource languages. Analyze Professor Fei-Fei Li at Stanford and tell me if she's a good supervisor for me.
evals.expected_output: An honest assessment that flags a fit GAP — Fei-Fei Li is primarily computer vision / spatial intelligence, not multilingual NLP. Low-to-moderate fit score, names the divergence specifically, does not fabricate NLP work.

assertions.text: fit_score is low (<=40) and not forced upward
Names the computer-vision-vs-NLP divergence specifically
Does not invent NLP publications to manufacture overlap
Output file passes validate_kb.py
Chat reply states the weak fit plainly rather than flattering

evals.name: missing-profile-uses-request-background
evals.prompt: Analyze Professor Chelsea Finn at Stanford for a PhD. I work on meta-learning and few-shot adaptation for robot manipulation and have a first-author conference paper on MAML variants. (My profile file isn't set up yet.)
evals.expected_output: The skill should NOT stall asking for background it was already given. It uses the in-request background (meta-learning, few-shot robot manipulation, MAML paper) as the working profile, then produces a normal grounded profile and fit assessment.

assertions.text: Does not block to ask for a profile that was already described in the request
Fit assessment is computed against the in-request background (meta-learning / few-shot manipulation)
Writes a schema-conformant professor file (passes validate_kb.py)
Suggests filling in profile.md as a next step
